questions.subject: Science
questions.prompt: Consider the following pairs regarding the classification of diabetes and their primary characteristics: 1. Type 1 Diabetes : Autoimmune destruction of insulin-producing beta cells 2. Type 2 Diabetes : Chronic undernutrition and low body weight 3. Type 5 Diabetes : Obesity and overnutrition 4. Gestational Diabetes : High blood glucose during pregnancy. How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Pair 1 is correctly matched: Type 1 diabetes involves the autoimmune destruction of insulin-producing beta cells. Pair 2 is incorrectly matched: Type 2 diabetes is mainly caused by obesity and overnutrition, not undernutrition. Pair 3 is incorrectly matched: Type 5 diabetes (also known as J-type diabetes) is associated with undernutrition and low weight. Pair 4 is correctly matched: Gestational diabetes occurs with high blood glucose during pregnancy. Therefore, only two pairs are correctly matched.
questions.takeaway: Type 5 diabetes is a newly recognized classification by the International Diabetes Federation (IDF) that specifically accounts for malnutrition-related diabetes, contrasting with the obesity-driven Type 2.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Malnutrition-related diabetes has been officially recognized and named type 5 diabetes... Also called J-type diabetes, is known to be associated with under nutrition and low weight. Other Types of Diabetes: Type 1: Autoimmune destruction of insulin-producing beta cells. Type 2: Mainly caused by obesity and over nutrition. Gestational: Occurs with high blood glucose during pregnancy.

questions.subject: Science
questions.prompt: With reference to the different types of diabetes, consider the following statements: 1. Type 5 diabetes, also known as J-type diabetes, is primarily associated with obesity and overnutrition. 2. Type 1 diabetes involves the autoimmune destruction of insulin-producing beta cells. 3. Gestational diabetes is characterized by high blood glucose levels occurring specifically during pregnancy. How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: Type 5 diabetes (J-type diabetes) is officially recognized as malnutrition-related diabetes and is associated with undernutrition and low weight, whereas Type 2 is associated with obesity. Statement 2 is correct: Type 1 diabetes is characterized by the autoimmune destruction of insulin-producing beta cells. Statement 3 is correct: Gestational diabetes occurs with high blood glucose during pregnancy. Thus, exactly two statements are correct.
questions.takeaway: Understanding the distinct etiologies of diabetes is crucial. While Type 1 is autoimmune and Type 2 is linked to overnutrition, the newly recognized Type 5 addresses cases stemming from chronic undernutrition.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Malnutrition-related diabetes has been officially recognized and named type 5 diabetes... Also called J-type diabetes, is known to be associated with under nutrition and low weight. Other Types of Diabetes: Type 1: Autoimmune destruction of insulin-producing beta cells... Gestational: Occurs with high blood glucose during pregnancy.

questions.subject: Science
questions.prompt: A medical researcher is analyzing different pathways that lead to elevated blood glucose levels in patients. Which of the following best describes the underlying physiological mechanism that distinguishes Type 1 diabetes from other common forms?

options.text: It is triggered by chronic undernutrition and low body mass index during early childhood.
It results from the autoimmune destruction of the insulin-producing beta cells in the pancreas.
It is a temporary metabolic dysfunction that occurs exclusively during pregnancy.
It develops primarily due to insulin resistance caused by severe obesity and overnutrition.

questions.explanation: Type 1 diabetes is characterized by the autoimmune destruction of insulin-producing beta cells. Option A describes the newly recognized Type 5 (malnutrition-related) diabetes. Option C describes Gestational diabetes. Option D describes Type 2 diabetes.
questions.takeaway: Different types of diabetes have distinct physiological triggers: autoimmune destruction (Type 1), obesity/overnutrition (Type 2), pregnancy (Gestational), and undernutrition (Type 5).
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Type 1: Autoimmune destruction of insulin-producing beta cells.

questions.subject: Science
questions.prompt: With reference to the global classification of diabetes, consider the following statements: 1. The International Diabetes Federation (IDF) recently recognized malnutrition-related diabetes as a distinct category known as Type 5 diabetes. 2. J-type diabetes is a localized term for gestational diabetes occurring in undernourished pregnant women. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct; the IDF officially recognized malnutrition-related diabetes as Type 5 at the World Diabetes Congress. Statement 2 is incorrect; J-type diabetes is another name for Type 5 (malnutrition-related diabetes), not gestational diabetes. Gestational diabetes specifically refers to high blood glucose during pregnancy.
questions.takeaway: Type 5 diabetes is officially recognized by the IDF and is synonymous with J-type or malnutrition-related diabetes.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Malnutrition-related diabetes has been officially recognized and named type 5 diabetes at International Diabetes Federation's (IDF) World Diabetes Congress. Also called J-type diabetes, is known to be associated with under nutrition and low weight.
